meaning: n.混杂物vt.使成为杂乱的一堆
examples: You end up with a mishmash of policies rather than a consistent national approach.
Japanese is turning into a confused mishmash of languages.
It's a mishmash of concepts from Behavioral Psychology and Behavior Modification.
The show was packed with a mishmash of highlights from sports around the world.
The reason is that price regulation has created a mishmash of conflicting policies and perverse incentives.
